marketState: neutral — near-term demand soft but extreme 8-16D heat forecast and historically short COT create medium-term upside potential
summary: Natural gas at $3.209 sits modestly above its fair value midpoint of $2.958 but well within the $2.46–$3.46 band, a premium sustained consistently across all four forward horizons, narrowing only to +$0.17 at the 21-day horizon as fair value rises toward current price. The dominant setup is a sharp contrast: near-term demand (1-7D) is -5% below the 5-year norm at the 38th percentile of the historical range, while the 8-16D demand outlook is +30% above 5-year norms and above the 5-year maximum — an extreme reading that is the single most important bullish catalyst in this dataset. Storage remains a structural headwind at +153 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish injection surprise of +25 Bcf last week, and daily and weekly technicals are bearish (50<200 EMA death cross, negative MACD on 1d and 4h). Seasonality shows a 69% probability of a higher close in 3 weeks but carries very low reliability (44 pp 10-90 spread), and managed-money net shorts sit at the 17th percentile of their 5-year range — extreme positioning that creates meaningful short-covering fuel if the heat wave verifies. Futures curve data is unavailable due to data feed failures, preventing term-structure assessment.

longTerm.invalidation: Storage surplus widens beyond +200 Bcf vs 5-year; the 8-16D extreme heat forecast reverses toward normal on successive model runs; or price breaks below $2.90 on volume, confirming the bearish technical trend.

shortTerm.rationale: Near-term demand is soft (-5% vs 5-year, 38th percentile) and daily/4h technicals are bearish (MACD negative, 50<200 EMA); however, the market is unlikely to break down cleanly given the extreme MM short positioning at the 17th percentile and the extraordinary 8-16D heat forecast approaching within days. The conflicting signals — near-term softness vs medium-term heat catalyst vs bearish technicals vs short-squeeze risk — make a high-confidence directional trade inadvisable without a cleaner setup.
shortTerm.trigger: Enter LONG if price pulls back to $3.00–$3.10 with 8-16D heat forecast maintained (better-risk entry for the heat trade), OR on a breakout above $3.30 with improving technical momentum signaling heat is being priced in. Enter SHORT only if price breaks below $2.95 (below fair-value midpoint) AND the 8-16D forecast moderates toward normal on successive model runs.
